revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Shinto shrine]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24699703697|time triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Fukuchi-jinja]][[ja:富知神社]] {{Expand Japanese|富知神社}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Fukuchi Shrine | native_name = 富知神社 | image = Fukuchi-jinja haiden-2.JPG | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Fukuchi Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|35.231186|138.606636}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|qid=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|az|Ooyamatsimu|de|Ōyamatsumi|en|Ōyamatsumi|fa|اویاماتسومی|fr|Ōyamatsumi|hr|O-Yama-Tsu-mi|ja|オオヤマツミ|ko|오야마쓰미노카미|mni|ꯑꯣꯌꯃꯠꯁꯨꯃꯤ|ru|Ооямацуми|simple|Ōyamatsumi|sv|Ōyamatsumi|zh|大山津見|qid=Q386563|1=Ōyamatsumi}} }} {{nihongo|'''Fukuchi Shrine'''|富知神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Suruga Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Q134917287|ltq=Q134917287|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} * {{ill|Q135022904|ltq=Q135022904|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} * {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|lt=Kokuhei-sha|qid=Q135160342|1=Kokuhei-sha}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Suruga Province|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Suruga Province|ja|駿河国の式内社一覧|qid=Q11668489|1=List of Shikinaisha in Suruga Province}} ** Series ordinal: 20 ** P155: {{ill|Q653180|ltq=Q653180|qid=Q653180|1=Fujisan Hongū Sengen Taisha}} ** P156: {{ill|Q135039634|ltq=Q135039634|qid=Q135039634|1=Marokono Shrine}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Fujinomiya, Shizuoka|lt=Fujinomiya|ar|فوجينوميا (شيزوكا)|arz|فوجينوميا، شيزوكا|azb|فوجی‌نومیا، شیزوئوکا|ceb|Fujinomiya (lungsod sa Hapon)|ce|Фудзиномия|cs|Fudžinomija|de|Fujinomiya|en|Fujinomiya, Shizuoka|envoyage|Fujinomiya|eo|Fuĝinomija|es|Fujinomiya|et|Fujinomiya|fa|فوجی‌نومیا، شیزوئوکا|fi|Fujinomiya|fr|Fujinomiya|hu|Fudzsinomija|hy|Ֆուձինոմիա|id|Fujinomiya, Shizuoka|it|Fujinomiya|ja|富士宮市|ko|후지노미야시|lt|Fudžinomija|mg|Fujinomiya|mzn|فوجی‌نومیا، شیزوئوکا|nl|Fujinomiya|no|Fujinomiya|pl|Fujinomiya|pt|Fujinomiya|ro|Fujinomiya|ru|Фудзиномия|sh|Fujinomija|sr|Фуџиномија|sv|Fujinomiya|tg|Фуҷиномия|th|ฟูจิโนมิยะ|tl|Fujinomiya|tr|Fujinomiya|tt|Фудзиномия|uk|Фудзіномія|vi|Fujinomiya, Shizuoka|war|Fujinomiya, Shizuoka|zh_classical|富士宮市|zh_min_nan|Huzinomiya-chhī|zh_yue|富士宮|zh|富士宮市|qid=Q496072|1=Fujinomiya, Shizuoka}} * {{ill|Q864170|ltq=Q864170|qid=Q864170|1=Suruga Province}} * {{ill|Fuji District, Shizuoka|lt=Fuji district|ceb|Fuji-gun|de|Fuji-gun|en|Fuji District, Shizuoka|fa|منطقه فوجی، شیزوئوکا|fr|District de Fuji|it|Distretto di Fuji|ja|富士郡|ko|후지군|nl|Fuji (district)|zh|富士郡|qid=Q1154962|1=Fuji District, Shizuoka}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|35.231186|138.606636}} == image (P18) == * Fukuchi-jinja haiden-2.JPG == dedicated to (P825) == * {{ill|Q386563|ltq=Q386563|qid=Q386563|1=Ōyamatsumi}} == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 181566 ** P3831: {{ill|Q135159299|ltq=Q135159299|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == legal form (P1454) == * {{ill|religious corporation|lt=religious corporation|ja|宗教法人|qid=Q7311343|1=religious corporation}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|ltq=Q17|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == name in kana (P1814) == * ふくちじんじゃhttps://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/download/zenken/index.html == postal code (P281) == * 418-0057https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/download/zenken/index.html == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 1080105003484 == Commons category (P373) == * Fukuchi-jinja == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Q135039633|ltq=Q135039633|qid=Q135039633|1=Fujino Shrine}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '静岡県富士宮市朝日町12-4', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '静岡県富士宮市朝日町', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Fukuchi-jinja}} [[JA:富知神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11457275}} == Japanese content == {{?? |?? = Fukuchi Shrine |?? = [[File:Fukuchi-jinja haiden-2.JPG|250px]]
Worship hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} |??? = {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|qid=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Fujinomiya|Q496072|qid=Q496072|1=Fujinomiya, Shizuoka}} Asahi-ch? 12-4 |??? = 35|??? = 13|??? = 52.50 |??? =138|??? = 36|??? = 23.68 |?? = {{ill|lt=?yamatsumi|Q386563|qid=Q386563|lt=?yamatsumi-no-Kami|1=Ōyamatsumi}} |?? = |?? = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}?small?{{ill|lt=candidate shrine|Q135022904|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}
former sessha of {{ill|lt=Fujisan Hong? Sengen Taisha|Q653180|qid=Q653180|1=Fujisan Hongū Sengen Taisha}} |?? = Emperor K?sh? year 2 (Imperial era year 187) |?? = |?? = |??? = |?? = {{ill|lt=September 19|Q2896|qid=Q2896|1=September 19}} |?? = |?? = Japan Shizuoka }} [[File:Fukuchi-jinja torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] '''Fukuchi Shrine''' (???????) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|qid=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Fujinomiya|Q496072|qid=Q496072|1=Fujinomiya, Shizuoka}}. It is an Engishiki-listed {{ill|lt=candidate shrine|Q135022904|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}, and a former sessha of {{ill|lt=Fujisan Hong? Sengen Taisha|Q653180|qid=Q653180|1=Fujisan Hongū Sengen Taisha}}. It is also known as "Fukuchi-sha," "Fukuchi My?jin-sha," "Fuji Shrine," and "Tomi Shrine""Place Names of Shizuoka Prefecture," entry for Fukuchi Shrine.. === Enshrined deity === * '''{{ill|lt=?yamatsumi|Q386563|qid=Q386563|lt=?yamatsumi-no-Kami|1=Ōyamatsumi}}''' (also called ?yamazumi-no-Mikoto)The shrine history text writes "?yamatsumi-no-Kami," while the precinct sign writes "?yamazumi-no-Mikoto." *: Father deity of {{ill|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|Q1781862|qid=Q1781862|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}} (deity of Sengen Taisha). === History === === Founding === Tradition says the shrine was founded in year 2 of {{ill|lt=Emperor K?sh?|Q312841|qid=Q312841|1=Emperor Kōshō}} (Imperial era 187)It is said this is recorded in the ''Suruga Fudoki'' ("History of Sengen Shrine," chapter 11, section on sessha and massha).. An Edo-period shrine tradition of Sengen Taisha (''Fuji Hong? Sengen Shaki'') states: "The site of the ?miya shrine was formerly the site of Fukuchi My?jin; Sengen Shrine was moved there. Fukuchi My?jin is the Engishiki-listed Fukuchi Shrine enshrining ?yamazumi-no-Mikoto." It says Fukuchi Shrine was the land guardian at the present Sengen Taisha site until Sengen Taisha moved there from Yamamiya (present {{ill|lt=Yamamiya Sengen Shrine|Q11467749|qid=Q11467749|1=Yamamiya Sengen Shrine}}) in Daid? 1 ({{ill|lt=806|Q30021|qid=Q30021|1=806}}). With that move, Fukuchi Shrine relocated to its current place, but has remained deeply connected to Sengen Taisha rituals as the local guardian deity. In {{ill|lt=Hirata Atsutane|Q2572749|qid=Q2572749|1=Hirata Atsutane}}'s ''{{ill|lt=Koshiden|Q11410975|qid=Q11410975|1=Koshiden}}'', it is written that this shrine existed in ?miya-ch? and was called Fukuchi Gongen. === Overview === In the mid-Heian ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|qid=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}'' {{ill|lt=Jinmy?ch?|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, "Fukuchi Shrine" is listed in Fuji District, Suruga Province, and this shrine is treated as one of its candidate shrines. Another candidate is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Fukuchi Rokusho Asama Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} (Asama Honch?, {{ill|lt=Fuji|Q328613|qid=Q328613|1=Fuji, Shizuoka}}). The reading in the register is marked as "Fuji." In the ''Suruga no kuni Jinmeich?,'' "Fukuchi Tenjin" appears at Senior Fifth Rank, Lower and "Fukuchi Chigi" at Junior Fifth Rank, UpperFrom shrine history text. See [{{NDLDC|991014/456}} Jinja Kakuroku, first volume] (National Diet Library Digital Collection), frames 456-457.. An article in ''{{ill|lt=Azuma Kagami|Q1042324|qid=Q1042324|1=Azuma Kagami}}'' for Bunji 2 ({{ill|lt=1186|Q19730|qid=Q19730|1=1186}}) contains "Fukuchi-sha of Kamimandokoro in Fuji territory." Since "Fuji territory" is considered Sengen Taisha estate, this suggests it was under Sengen Taisha influence by then. Afterward, the shrine continued as a sessha of Sengen Taisha, and rituals were managed by the shrine official Fukuchi Day? of Sengen Taisha. Since the appointment of Yamada Jinzabur? in Genki 3 ({{ill|lt=1572|Q6670|qid=Q6670|1=1572}}), the Yamada family is thought to have inherited the role of Fukuchi Day? until Meiji. It is confirmed as a sessha of Sengen Taisha until around Meiji, but is now effectively independent"History of Sengen Shrine," chapter 11, section on sessha and massha.. === Annual festival === The annual festival day is September 19. The festival is now run by a rotating duty among four parish groups (Fuku, Kanga, Kandate, and Hagoromo). Since Reiwa 1, a parish youth association has organized children's mikoshi processions in thanks to the shrine deity and for community prosperity. From Reiwa 4 onward, the annual festival has been held on the weekend nearest September 19 in line with that procession. [[????:???????.jpg|?????|{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shink?sai|1=UNKNOWN}}]] Reiwa 2 duty: Fuku district - {{ill|lt=tekomai|Q11496936|qid=Q11496936|1=Tegomai}}, shrine-name banner, drums, children's {{ill|lt=mikoshi|Q979940|qid=Q979940|1=Mikoshi}}. Reiwa 3 duty: Fuku district - shrine-name banner, drums, children's mikoshi. Reiwa 4 duty: Kanga district - {{ill|lt=takahari lantern|UNKNOWN|lt=takahari}}, tekomai, shrine-name banner, drums, children's mikoshi. Reiwa 5 duty: Kandate district - takahari, tekomai, shrine-name banner, drums, children's mikoshi. Reiwa 6 duty: Hagoromo district - takahari, tekomai, shrine-name banner, drums, takahari, {{ill|lt=hayashi|Q4171186|qid=Q4171186|1=Hayashi (music)}} troupe, children's mikoshi, main shrine mikoshi. === Precinct === File:Fukuchi-jinja honden.JPG|Main hall File:Fukuchi-jinja haiden-1.JPG|Worship hall === Sessha and massha === * Mitsumine Shrine - enshrined to the left facing the main hall. == Considerations == [[File:Wakutama-ike-2.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{ill|lt=Wakutama Pond|Q11563392|qid=Q11563392|1=Wakutamaike}} (within Sengen Taisha precincts)]] Regarding "Fukuchi" in the shrine name, a relation to the name of Mt. Fuji has been pointed out. Historical documents preserve two old-name systems for Mt. Fuji"Nihon no Kamigami," entry for Fujisan Hong? Sengen Taisha.: * Fushi-type: ?? / ?? (''{{ill|lt=Man'y?sh?|Q834321|qid=Q834321|1=Man'yōshū}}''), ?? (''{{ill|lt=Hitachi no kuni fudoki|Q11481687|qid=Q11481687|1=Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki}}'') * Fuchi-type: ?? (this shrine), ?? (''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Wamy?sh?|1=UNKNOWN}}'', Tsuru District, Kai Province) The "Fuchi" old name is said to derive from water worship. In ''Fuji Hong? Sengen Shaki,'' this shrine is said to have originally stood at the current Sengen Taisha site, where spring water emerges at {{ill|lt=Wakutama Pond|Q11563392|qid=Q11563392|1=Wakutamaike}}; worship there likely centered on that spring as "wakutama" (gushing sacred spirit). By contrast, the old name "Asama" for the Sengen deity is considered a word for "volcano," as seen in {{ill|lt=Mount Aso|Q733710|qid=Q733710|1=Mount Aso}}, {{ill|lt=Mount Asama|Q242730|qid=Q242730|1=Mount Asama}}, and {{ill|lt=Asahidake (Tochigi)|Q11517490|qid=Q11517490|lt=Asahidake|1=Mount Asahi}}. It is suggested that the need to pacify Fuji eruptions led fire-god worship to overtake water-god worship. The earliest record of Fuji eruption in official history appears in ''{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihongi|Q611399|qid=Q611399|1=Shoku Nihongi}}'', Ten'? 1 ({{ill|lt=781|Q31175|qid=Q31175|1=781}}), seventh month; earlier records describe Fuji only as a peaceful mountain, implying major eruptions had not yet occurred. From this perspective, the ritual shift traditionally dated to Daid? 1 ({{ill|lt=806|Q30021|qid=Q30021|1=806}}) is interpreted as symbolizing a transition from "water-deity worship (Fuchi)" to "fire-deity worship (Asama)" on Mt. Fuji. === Local information === '''Address''' * {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|qid=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Fujinomiya|Q496072|qid=Q496072|1=Fujinomiya, Shizuoka}} Asahi-ch? 12-4 '''Access''' * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=Central Japan Railway Company|Q513679|qid=Q513679|1=Central Japan Railway Company}} (JR Central), {{ill|lt=Minobu Line|Q162747|qid=Q162747|1=Minobu Line}}, {{ill|lt=Nishi-Fujinomiya Station|Q7040330|qid=Q7040330|1=Nishi-Fujinomiya Station}} (about 12 minutes on foot) === Notes === {{Reflist}} === References === * ''Brief History of Fukuchi Shrine'' (shrine history text) * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Place Names of Shizuoka Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|qid=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), Fujinomiya City entry for Fukuchi Shrine * Hideo End?, ''Mysteries and Strange Tales of Mt. Fuji'', Shizushin Shinsho, 2007. * {{Cite book|??|author=|translator=|editor=Eiji Hirano|year=1987|chapter=|title=Fuji Sengen Faith|series=History of Popular Religion Vol.16|publisher=Y?zankaku Publishing|isbn=4639006578|ref=}} * {{Cite book|??|author1=Naokazu Miyaji|authorlink1=????|author2=Sabur? Hirono|translator=|editor=Asama Shrine|year=1929|chapter=|title=History of Sengen Shrine|series=Studies of Fuji|publisher=Konkoin|isbn=|ref=}} * ''Asama Monjo San'' edited by Asama Shrine Office, Meicho Kank?kai, 1973. *{{Cite book|??|author=Kan'ichi Nomoto|authorlink=????|editor=Ken'ichi Tanigawa|editor-link=????|year=1987|chapter=Fujisan Hong? Sengen Taisha|title=Nihon no Kamigami - Shrines and Sacred Sites - 10 T?kai|publisher=Hakusuisha|isbn=4560022208|ref=}} * Ueda Kazutoshi et al. (eds.), ''Collected Works of Hirata Atsutane'', vol.4 (Koshi 4), Naigai Shoseki, 1933. National Diet Library Digital Collection https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1051875 (accessed 2025-05-27) === See also === {{Commonscat|Fukuchi-jinja}} *{{ill|lt=Fujisan Hong? Sengen Taisha|Q653180|qid=Q653180|1=Fujisan Hongū Sengen Taisha}} {{?? ?}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Fukuchi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Fukuchi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Suruga Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Suruga Province]] [[Category:History of Fujinomiya]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Fujinomiya]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]]
